lead_profile_text: 6-bedroom detached house over 3 storeys, Ward End, Birmingham. Family moved out 6 months ago; property vacant and costing £1,500/month. Fully furnished. Certain areas to remain locked (office, conservatory with leak). 1GB Virgin fibre installed. Gas and electrical certificates still needed. Follow-up from entry 042.
emotional_profile: High-intent, financially motivated with a clear urgency driver — £1,500/month ongoing costs on a vacant property. Already ruled out selling and traditional rental. Only risk is procedural delay (certificates, conservatory, kickoff scheduling), not decision reluctance.
delay_trigger_phrases: Gas and electrical safety certificates not yet in place
Conservatory with a leak — certain areas will remain locked
target: progress ASAP due to ongoing monthly costs
delay_timeline: ASAP — procedural only, kickoff after agreement signed
green_light_trigger_phrases: I don't want to sell or do a traditional rental

top_objections.text: Wants certain areas locked — office and conservatory with leak
top_objections.zac_response: Standard procedure — three key safes recommended; locked rooms excluded from guest access at setup.

top_objections.text: Wants weekly property inspections between guest stays
top_objections.zac_response: To be discussed at kickoff call — expectation management needed on inspection frequency.

top_objections.text: Gas and electrical safety certificates not yet in place
top_objections.zac_response: Owner action required before listing — cannot go live without these certificates.

top_questions.text: Can certain rooms remain locked and inaccessible to guests?
top_questions.answer: Yes — standard practice. Three key safes recommended: guests, cleaners, landlord access. Locked rooms simply excluded from the listing.

top_questions.text: What inspections can be carried out between guest stays?
top_questions.answer: To be discussed at kickoff — regular inspections are possible but scope and frequency needs aligning with cleaning schedule.

what_moved_them: £1,500/month ongoing vacancy cost — STL clearly and immediately solves his financial problem
Property fully furnished — no furnishing costs, fast path to listing
13%+VAT offer with a clear deadline creating a concrete decision point
Case study reference: 3-bed Manchester property achieved £56k gross / £36k net in first year

conversion_gaps.gap: Gas and electrical safety certificates not in place — cannot go live without these
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Chase Shahid to arrange certificates immediately; provide list of local approved engineers if helpful.

conversion_gaps.gap: Conservatory leak unresolved — guest access boundary needs confirming
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Confirm at kickoff that conservatory stays locked; plan for repair timeline that won't delay listing.

offer_framing: 13%+VAT expires 23 June 2026. Framed squarely against £1,500/month ongoing vacancy cost. ASAP urgency to stop the financial bleed.
zac_close_phrase: Sign before 23 June to lock in the 13% rate and we move forward immediately to kickoff
zac_income_intro: Average net revenue £4,088/month (£49,060/year) after all fees. Nightly rate £379 at 68% occupancy. Range: £2,912 in February to £4,677 in October.
zac_transition_phrases: Family moved out 6 months ago — that's £9,000 in ongoing costs while the property sits empty
Three key safes: guests, cleaners, landlord access — locked rooms simply don't exist to guests
zac_reassurance_phrases: Property already fully furnished — fastest possible path to going live
Locked rooms are completely standard — we simply exclude them from the listing
zac_analogies: 3-bed Manchester property: £56k gross / £36k net in first year — comparable trajectory for a large property
